PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk Reports Earnings Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
PT Bank Negara Indonesia (Persero) Tbk reported earnings results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the nine months, the company reported net income was IDR 15,115,133 million compared to IDR 16,308,385 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was IDR 405 compared to IDR 437 a year ago.

Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was IDR 405 compared to IDR 437 a year ago.

data.title: Super League announces follow on close of private placement offering, securing total of $17.9 million
Aeromexico asks US court to block Trump order forcing end to Delta joint venture
data.description:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) -Aeromexico on Friday asked the 11th Circuit Court of Appeals to halt a Trump administration order forcing it to unwind a joint venture with Delta Air Lines by January 1 that lets the carriers coordinate scheduling, pricing and capacity decisions for U.S.-Mexico flights.

Aeromexico said it would face substantial costs to unwind the joint venture that it could not recover even if a court later upheld the arrangement. The U.S. Transportation Department in September ordered an end to the nearly nine-year-old joint venture as part of a number of actions aimed at Mexican aviation, citing competition concerns.

(Reporting by David Shepardson)

data.title: US Equity Markets Close Higher After Milder Inflation Data
Fed Is in 'No Man's Land' Right Now, KPMG's Swonk Says
data.description:
KPMG Chief Economist Diane Swonk reacts to the latest inflation report and discusses what it means for Federal Reserve monetary policy on "Bloomberg The Close." The core consumer price index increased 0.2% from August, according to Bureau of Labor Statistics data. That was the slowest pace in three months.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

data.title: Ohio County Authority Selling $77 Million of Multifamily Housing Revenue Bonds
data.description:
By Chris Wack


The Columbus-Franklin County Finance Authority in Ohio is offering $77 million of municipal bonds for a multifamily rental facility in the state capital of Columbus.

Proceeds from the Multifamily Housing Revenue Bonds Series 2025 will be used to help pay for a portion of the cost of buying, constructing and equipping a 321-unit project, known as The Landmark on Scioto, according to a document posted Thursday on MuniOS.

The project will include a community room, fitness center, laundry facility, office, maintenance area and outdoor paved parking lot. Construction will begin after the bonds are issued, and should take about 30 months.

Interest on the bonds is payable starting on June 1 and again on Dec. 1. The project will include a community room, fitness center, laundry facility, office, maintenance area and outdoor paved parking lot. Construction will begin after the bhonds are issued, and should take about 30 months.

Interest on the bonds is payable starting on June 1 and again on Dec. 1. The securities will mature in 2044.

The bonds, principal and interest are special limited obligations of the authority, and are payable solely from and secured by a pledge of net revenue and additional funds on a parity with a pledge securing all outstanding bonds.

The authority is political subdivision of the state of Ohio, authorized to enhance, foster or promote economic development, housing, recreation, culture or research.

Moody's assigned the bonds a rating of Aa1.

KeyBanc Capital Markets is listed as the offering's manager.


Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 1622ET
